name: The stupid monk recovers the stolen flocks. A nobleman steals the abbot's flocks, saying that the monks have no use for them since they eat no meat. The most learned of the monks tries to recover them, but without success. The most stupid is then sent. Asked to dinner, he eats till he can hold no more. He tells the nobleman that he ate as much as possible since he could take back with him only what he had in his stomach. The nobleman pleased with the reply returns the flocks.
section: L140. The unpromising surpasses the promising.
bibliographies: Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 61; Alphabet No. 718; Mensa Philosophica No. 187.
additional_description: A nobleman steals the abbot's flocks, saying that the monks have no use for them since they eat no meat. The most learned of the monks tries to recover them, but without success. The most stupid is then sent. Asked to dinner, he eats till he can hold no more. He tells the nobleman that he ate as much as possible since he could take back with him only what he had in his stomach. The nobleman pleased with the reply returns the flocks.

name: Marriage of poor boy and rich girl.

name: Fool wins beautiful woman as wife.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 530B*; India: Thompson-Balys.

name: Mercenary soldier (exile) accepted lover of princess.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

name: Lowly heroine marries prince (king).
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

Polynesia: Kirtley: Tuamotus: Stimson, n.d., Z-G. 13/346; Rarotonga: Te Ariki, 1921, 208.

name: Lowly boy becomes king. (Most references to L161 apply here).
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

additional_description: (Most references to +L161 apply here).

name: Lowly successful hero invites king and humbles him.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

name: Lowly successful soldier invites general and humbles him.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

name: Despised boy wins race.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).
bibliographies: N. Am. Indian: *Thompson Tales 327f. nn. 185f. (many references), (Zuñi): *Benedict II 336. Polynesia: Kirtley: Hawaii: Fornander, 1918, V, 126ff.415

name: Despised boy wins gambling game.
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

name: [First Edition: L190. Unpromising hero (heroine) – miscellaneous.]
section: L160. Success of the unpromising hero (heroine).

name: Modesty brings reward.
Modest choice best.
bibliographies: Types 480, 580. See also references to L211 and L221. Scala Celi 26a No. 171; *Roberts 177, 198; Spanish: Espinosa Jr. Nos. 71, 74, 139, 191; Italian: Basile Pentamerone III No. 10; Icelandic: Boberg; Japanese: Ikeda; N. Am. Indian (Zuñi): Benedict II 340; Africa: Werner African 196, 205; Cape Verde Islands: Parsons MAFLS XV (1) 277 No. 91; West Indies: Flowers 559.

name: Modest choice: three caskets type. Objects from which choice is to be made are hidden in caskets (or the like). The worst looking casket proves to be the best choice.
bibliographies: Type 480; *Cox 501ff.; Cosquin Lorraine No. 17; *Chauvin III 99 No. 4; *Crane Vitry 153 No. 47; *Oesterley No. 251; *Roberts 200; *Cosquin Contes indiens 527; Boccaccio Decameron X No. 1 (Lee 294); *Ward II 122; Herbert III 196; *Wesselski Märchen 213 No. 18; *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 836; *Fb "skål" III 352a. North Carolina: Brown Collection I 633; Spanish Exempla: Keller; Italian Novella: *Rotunda; India: *Thompson-Balys; Chinese: Graham; Japanese: Anesaki 320; N. Am. Indian (Arikara): Dorsey CI XVII No. 16, (Nez Percé): Spinden JAFL XXI 156, (Carib): Alexander Lat. Am. 264; Malay: V. Ronkel Catalogus der Maleische Handschriften te Batavia (Den Haag, 1909) 48; Africa (Angola): Chatelain 121 No. 10, 229 No. 41, (Benga): Nassau 225 No. 33.
additional_description: Objects from which choice is to be made are hidden in caskets (or the like). The worst looking casket proves to be the best choice.

name: Choice among several gifts. The worst horse, armor, or the like proves best.
bibliographies: *Fb "hest" I 598b; Child II 444f., 450, 453f.; German: Grimm No. 57; Spanish: Espinosa Jr. Nos. 62, 64; Jewish: *Neuman; India: *Thompson-Balys; Chinese: Graham; N. Am. Indian: Thompson CColl II 341, (Arikara): Dorsey CI XVII No. 16; West Indies: Flowers 559. Polynesia: Kirtley: Tonga: Caillot, 1914, 255f.; Samoa: Fraser, 1897, 71; Krämer, 1902, I, 413n. 5; Powell-Pratt, 1892, 243.
additional_description: The worst horse, armor, or the like proves best.

name: Saint, offered any gift from God, chooses (virginity and) wisdom.
Solomon, offered any gift from God, chooses wisdom. Granted wisdom and wealth.
additional_description: Granted wisdom and wealth.

name: Hero prefers fame to long life.
"Fame (honor) is more enduring than life."

Modest choice proves good (simple unique privilege). Girl offered reward of five villages chooses rather to be only one on certain night to be allowed to have light in her house and to keep all animals who enter as hers. All kinds of livestock come.
additional_description: Girl offered reward of five villages chooses rather to be only one on certain night to be allowed to have light in her house and to keep all animals who enter as hers. All kinds of livestock come.

name: Poor girl chosen rather than the rich. Treasure follows.
bibliographies: Chauvin III 103 No. 13; Spanish Exempla: Keller.
additional_description: Treasure follows.

name: Modest choice best: wife chosen from crowd of women – only one poorly dressed.
Choice of ugliest girl as bride.
Old chosen rather than new. Fortunate choice.
additional_description: Fortunate choice.

name: Unpromising magic object chosen. Hero refuses to take one that cries out "take me!"
bibliographies: Roberts 204; Tonga: Beckwith Myth 25, Gifford 20; Africa (Benga): Nassau 114 No. 11; Jamaica: *Beckwith MAFLS XVII 269 No. 81.
additional_description: Hero refuses to take one that cries out "take me!

name: Poor game proves rich.
bibliographies: N. Am. Indian (California): Gayton and Newman 83.

name: Accustomed rags preferred to new garments. A Brahmin returns home to find a palace instead of a cottage; he recognizes his wife only after she throws off her jewels and ornaments to stand before him in her old rags.
additional_description: A Brahmin returns home to find a palace instead of a cottage; he recognizes his wife only after she throws off her jewels and ornaments to stand before him in her old rags.

name: Former poverty chosen over new riches. Weaver laments loss of water vessel. Offered many new, but prefers old and modest life.
additional_description: Weaver laments loss of water vessel. Offered many new, but prefers old and modest life.

name: Modest request best.
bibliographies: *Cox 480; *BP II 232; *MacCulloch Childhood 191; N. Am. Indian: *Thompson Tales 276 n. 18a.

name: Modest request: present from the journey. Asked what her father shall bring her as a present, the heroine chooses a modest gift. It is usually a flower but sometimes does not turn out to be such a simple gift after all (golden cloak, golden apple).
bibliographies: *Type 425; *Fb "rose" III 80a, "guldæble" I 515b, "guldrok" I 514b; *Hdwb. d. Märchens I 206b; Tegethoff 11; Italian Basile Pentamerone II No. 8; India: Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: Asked what her father shall bring her as a present, the heroine chooses a modest gift. It is usually a flower but sometimes does not turn out to be such a simple gift after all (golden cloak, golden apple).

name: Present from the journey: what you first see.
Modest choice: parting gift. Small gift with blessing preferred to large gift with parent's curse.
bibliographies: *Köhler-Bolte I 188; BP I 214 n. 1; Gunkel Das Märchen im alten Testament (Tübingen, 1921) 100f.; Campbell Tales I 220 No. 13; Lang English Fairy Tales 136 No. 23; Gipsy: Aichele Zigeunermärchen 289 No. 69; Jewish: Neuman; Cape Verde Islands: Parsons MAFLS XV (1) 110, 122.
additional_description: Small gift with blessing preferred to large gift with parent's curse.

name: Modest choice for parting gift – money or counsels. Counsels chosen.
bibliographies: Jewish: bin Gorion Born Judas III 100, 304.
additional_description: Counsels chosen.

name: Modest parting gift best – meat or bones. Bones thrown to pursuing dogs delay them and allow escape; not so with meat.
additional_description: Bones thrown to pursuing dogs delay them and allow escape; not so with meat.

name: Modest choice for parting gift: when offered money man takes magic stick.
Modest choice of parting gift: magic iron measure chosen.
Hero refuses reward. Rides away without it.
bibliographies: *Types 300, 303; Italian: Basile Pentamerone I No. 7; Icelandic: Boberg.
additional_description: Rides away without it.

name: Modest business plans best.
Beggar with small bag surpasses the one with the large. Latter refuses all but large donations; gets none.
bibliographies: Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 607; *Crane Vitry 168 No. 77; Alphabet No. 607.
additional_description: Latter refuses all but large donations; gets none.

name: Modesty brings reward – miscellaneous.

Prosperity forever or for a day? King asks prince whether he has secret of prosperity forever or a day. Prince says "forever" and is captured. Later his wife, asked same question, says "for a day" and is honored.
additional_description: King asks prince whether he has secret of prosperity forever or a day. Prince says "forever" and is captured. Later his wife, asked same question, says "for a day" and is honored.

name: Triumph of the weak.
bibliographies: Chauvin II 204 No. 59; Irish myth: Cross.

name: Hermes distributes wit. Gives everyone the same measure of wit, so that the smaller are more clever than the large.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 35; Halm Aesop No. 150.
additional_description: Gives everyone the same measure of wit, so that the smaller are more clever than the large.

name: Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Weak (small) hero overcomes large fighter.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
bibliographies: *Type 328; A. Stender-Petersen Acta Jutlandica VI (1934) 166ff.; Irish myth: Cross; English: Child II 35ff.; Icelandic: *Boberg; Spanish Exempla: Keller; Italian Novella: Rotunda; Japanese: Anesaki 311; Jewish: Neuman, I Samuel 17:23 (David and Goliath); N. Am. Indian (Southern Paiute, Shivwits): Lowie JAFL XXXVII 150 No. 17; Guatamala Indian (Quiche): Alexander Lat. Am. 168ff.; Tuamotu: Beckwith Myth 476. Polynesia: Kirtley: Tuamotus: Beckwith, 1940, 476; Cooks: Gill, 1876, 54ff., 67ff.; Hawaii: Beckwith, 1940, 465; Fornander, 1916, IV, 166ff.; Rice, 1923, 93; Westervelt, 1915a, 178; Samoa: Brown, 1915, 174f.; Tikopia: Firth, 1961, 115.

name: Sick hero overcomes antagonist.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Poorly-armed hero overcomes well-armed by strategy.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Poor prince overcomes king.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Little innocent girl is able to drive giant out of land.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Small boy overcomes enraged gorilla.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Little strong man defeats giant in race.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Small animal overcomes large.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Bird flies into large animal's ear and kills him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
bibliographies: *Type 228; *BP II 437; Japanese: Ikeda; Indonesia: DeVries's list No. 115.

name: Mouse runs into buffalo's ear and overcomes him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Mouse torments bull who cannot catch him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Fox burns tree in which eagle has nest. Revenges theft of cub.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
additional_description: Revenges theft of cub.

name: Mother ape burns bear. Revenges theft of her young.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
additional_description: Revenges theft of her young.

name: Lark causes elephant to fall over precipice.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
bibliographies: Chauvin II 81 No. 1; India: Thompson-Balys; Buddhist myth: Malalasekera II 771; Japanese: Ikeda.

name: Elephant killed by mouse who runs up open end of trunk to head and there smears poison over his brain.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Insects worry large animal to despair or death.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *48 (ET 76), 120 (ST 301); Spanish: Espinosa Jr. Nos. 187, 209; Japanese: Ikeda; Indonesia: DeVries's list Nos. 132, 137.

name: Dungbeetle keeps destroying eagle's eggs. Eagle at last goes to the sky and lays eggs in Zeus's lap. The dungbeetle causes Zeus to shake his apron and break the eggs.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *51 (ET 106), 77 (ET 432), 113 (ST 237), 114 (ST 249); Halm Aesop No. 7.
additional_description: Eagle at last goes to the sky and lays eggs in Zeus's lap. The dungbeetle causes Zeus to shake his apron and break the eggs.

name: Fish pricks monster with fins and defeats him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Falcon attacks eagle repeatedly and defeats him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Mice overcome camel.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Lizard defeats leopard.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Rabbit slays rhinoceros.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Hedgehog defeats tiger by jumping into tiger's mouth and tormenting him.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Ants overcome serpent.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Small animals dupe larger into trap.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Offended rats gnaw saddle girths of king's horses so that he is defeated in battle.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Mice win war with woodcutters.
section: L310. Weak overcomes strong in conflict.

name: Easy escape of weak (small).
Little fishes escape from the net. The large are caught.
bibliographies: *Type 253; *BP III 355; Wienert FFC LVI 66 (ET 299), 113 (ST 240); Halm Aesop No. 26.
additional_description: The large are caught.

name: Mice escape into their holes; weasels cannot follow them.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 48 (ET 65), *113 (ST 241); Halm Aesop No. 291.

name: Hummingbird can see fowler's net; eagle is caught in spite of his boasts of good eyesight.
bibliographies: *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 290; Rumanian: Schullerus FFC LXXVIII No. 254*.

name: Mildness triumphs over violence.
Mildness triumphs over violence: queen advises husband to use kindness to enemies. This wins them over where war failed.
additional_description: This wins them over where war failed.

name: Saint uses kind words to pagan priest who has just smitten a Christian. This causes pagan to repent. Conversion follows.
additional_description: This causes pagan to repent. Conversion follows.

name: Contest of wind and sun. Sun by warmth causes traveler to remove coat, while wind by violent blowing causes him to pull it closer around him.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 43 (ET 7), 80 (ET 457), 136 (ST 419); Halm Aesop No. 82; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 3900; Estonian: Loorits Grundzüge I 381ff.; Russian: Andrejev No. 298*; Indonesia: DeVries's list Nos. 95, 140. Cf. Halm No. 414.
additional_description: Sun by warmth causes traveler to remove coat, while wind by violent blowing causes him to pull it closer around him.

name: Contest between wind (rain) and sun. Rain also tries unsuccessfully like wind.
additional_description: Rain also tries unsuccessfully like wind.

name: Sun cursed by man for its burning rays, wind for its hot breath, but moon is blessed for its soft, cool, and beautiful light.
Mild brother triumphs over warlike. Survives him and inherits property.
additional_description: Survives him and inherits property.

name: Priest who gives mild penances succeeds where others fail.
bibliographies: Scala Celi 48a No. 272; Alphabet No. 183; Herbert III 505, 570; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Goldsmith gives money to one who addressed him as friend (the goldsmith had no friends because he has cheated everybody).
Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.

name: Needle kills an elk. Slips into his stomach.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Slips into his stomach.

name: Reed pricks and drives away dog that urinates on it.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.

name: Mouse stronger than wall, wind, mountain.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.
bibliographies: *DeCock Volkssage 31ff.; BP I 148 n. 2; Wesselski Mönchslatein 82 No. 75; Chauvin II 97 No. 55; Archiv f. d. Studium d. neueren Sprachen LXXXI 265; Germania II 481; Basset RTP VII 394ff.; Basset Contes Berbères 95f; *Bødker Exempler 297 No. 60. Spanish: Keller, Espinosa III Nos. 275–277; Jewish: Neuman; India: Thompson-Balys; Japanese: Ikeda.

name: Mosquitoes sting King Pharaoh and show they are stronger than the man who cannot escape them.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.

name: Only love to offer. Bride asks suitors what they have to offer her. Poor youth who has nothing but love to offer gets her.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Bride asks suitors what they have to offer her. Poor youth who has nothing but love to offer gets her.

name: Slow flying swan lasts longer than speedy crow in flying.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.

name: Frog, tortoise, fish each tell of how long they expect to live. Frog alone does not expect to live to a hundred and ten years and alone escapes fisherman's net.
section: L390. Triumph of the weak – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Frog alone does not expect to live to a hundred and ten years and alone escapes fisherman's net.

name: Pride brought low.
Proud ruler (deity) humbled.
Proud king humbled: realizes that pomp, possessions, power are all of short duration.
King, defeated in battle, obliged to flee without money.
Boasting king insulted by crow dropping filth on him.
Defeated king must be peddler or beggar.
King overthrown and made servant.
Ruler enslaved.
bibliographies: India: *Thompson-Balys. Polynesia: Kirtley: Hawaii: Fornander, 1916, IV, 134.

name: Queen forced to become a courtesan.

Proud king displaced by angel. (King in the bath.) While the king is in the bath (or hunting) an angel in his form takes his place. The king is repulsed on all sides until he repents of his haughtiness.
bibliographies: *Type 757; **Varnhagen Ein indisches Märchen auf seine Wanderung durch die asiatischen und europäischen Literaturen (Berlin, 1882); Chauvin II 161 No. 51; *Wesselski Märchen 237 No. 49; Hibbard 58ff.; *Herbert III 202; *Oesterley No. 59; *Andraea Anglia Beiblatt XIII 302; *von der Hagen III cxv; bin Gorion Born Judas III 47ff., 299f.; *Goebel Jüdische Motive im Märchenhafte Erzählungsgut (Gleiwitz, 1932) 89ff. Italian Novella: *Rotunda; Icelandic: Boberg; India: *Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: (King in the bath.) While the king is in the bath (or hunting) an angel in his form takes his place. The king is repulsed on all sides until he repents of his haughtiness.

name: Rich man made poor to punish pride. He boasts that God has no power to make him poor. While he is at church, his property burns and he returns home poor.
bibliographies: Type 836; Irish: Beal XXI 336, O'Suilleabhain 122; Jewish: bin Gorion Born Judas II 249; India: Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: He boasts that God has no power to make him poor. While he is at church, his property burns and he returns home poor.

name: Woman casts ring into sea boasting that it is as impossible for her to become poor as for the ring to be found. Ring is found in fish: she becomes poor.
bibliographies: W. Jones Finger-Ring Lore (London, 1898) 440; Finnish-Swedish: Wessman 19 No. 178.
additional_description: Ring is found in fish: she becomes poor.

name: Proud inscriptions sole remains of powerful king.
bibliographies: *Chauvin V 33 No. 16; Spanish Exempla: Keller. Cf. Shelley's "Ozymandias".

name: King vainly forbids tide to rise. (Canute.)
additional_description: (Canute.)

name: King vainly attempts to measure the height of the sky and the depth of the sea.
God punishes David for his pride in the number of his subjects. An angel is sent to kill his people. Desists only when David repents.
additional_description: An angel is sent to kill his people. Desists only when David repents.

name: King commanded to wear enemy's shoes on his shoulders as sign of submission.
Proud king humbled when imprisoned by enemies.
God finds that his statue sells at low price. He prices it in a statue shop and finds that his price is lower than other gods.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 80 (ET 455), 94 (ST 77); Halm Aesop No. 137.
additional_description: He prices it in a statue shop and finds that his price is lower than other gods.

name: King shown he is less powerful than God. In spite of all his plans, his servant recovers the lost (exchanged) treasure.
additional_description: In spite of all his plans, his servant recovers the lost (exchanged) treasure.

name: Proud ruler (deity) humbled – miscellaneous.
Goose boasts superiority to mushroom. Both served up at same meal.
additional_description: Both served up at same meal.

name: King (prince) becomes beggar.
Overweening ambition punished.
Overweening ambition punished. Man sets self up as a god.
additional_description: Man sets self up as a god.

name: Attempt to fly to heaven punished. Car supported by eagles.
additional_description: Car supported by eagles.

name: Attempt to climb to heaven punished.
Peter acts as God for a day: tires of bargain. A girl takes her goat to pasture and leaves him: "My God care for you!" Peter must run everywhere after the goat.
additional_description: A girl takes her goat to pasture and leaves him: "My God care for you! " Peter must run everywhere after the goat.

name: Man who has never known unhappiness or want is swallowed up by earth with all his household.
Dream (prophecy) of future greatness causes banishment (imprisonment).
Poor man aspires to high office: made a cook.
Arrogance repaid.
Arrogant mistress repaid in kind by her lover.
bibliographies: *Type 900; *BP I 443ff.; **Philippson König Drosselbart FFC L; Krappe Études ital. II 141ff.; *Gigas "Et eventyrs vandring" Litteratur og Historie (København, 1902); *Fb "bejler" IV 31b; Icelandic: Boberg; West Indies: Flowers 560.

name: Haughty mistress makes extravagant demands of lover: repaid. (Glove and the Lion.)
bibliographies: *Shearin MLN XXVI 113; *Buchanan Modern Language Review IV 183 n. 1; Krappe MLN XXXIV 16; Italian Novella: *Rotunda; N. Am. Indian: *Thompson Tales 349 n. 256.
additional_description: (Glove and the Lion.)

name: Scorned lover poses as rich man and cheats his scornful mistress.
bibliographies: Boccaccio Decameron VIII No. 10 (Lee 266); Italian Novella: *Rotunda.

name: Sneering princess is impregnated by magic.
Impoverished husband begs from wife's new husband. He has formerly refused this man charity.
bibliographies: *Chauvin II 174 No. 16, VIII 180 No. 212; *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin I 263 N. 232; *Basset 1001 Contes II 305.
additional_description: He has formerly refused this man charity.

name: Cruel brothers brought to beg charity from abused sister.
Impoverished father begs from daughter he has banished: recognized.
Impoverished father begs from his prosperous daughter whom he has made to marry a poor man.
Impoverished husband begs from wife he has formerly expelled.
Impoverished youngest brother comes to elders in search of work.
Arrogant farmer allows none to ride his precious horse without permission. He kills the man who does it, but is in revenge deprived of most of his goods.
bibliographies: Icelandic: Hrafnkels saga Freysgods ch. 2ff. (ed. F. S. Cawley) (Cambridge, Mass., 1932).
additional_description: He kills the man who does it, but is in revenge deprived of most of his goods.

name: Self-righteousness punished.
Self-righteous hermit must do penance. He has said of a condemned man that he deserves his punishment.
bibliographies: *Types 756A, 756B; BP III 463ff.; *Andrejev FFC LXIX 160ff., 250ff.; Wesselski Mönchslatein 21 No. 16; Alphabet No. 206; Irish: O'Suilleabhain 99, Beal XXI 332; Spanish: Espinosa II Nos 79f.
additional_description: He has said of a condemned man that he deserves his punishment.

name: Self-righteous monk rebuked by abbot. Abbot tells him to search his own heart to see if he is free of sin before attacking others.
additional_description: Abbot tells him to search his own heart to see if he is free of sin before attacking others.

name: Self-righteous woman punished. She has passed judgment on a girl who has a bastard.
additional_description: She has passed judgment on a girl who has a bastard.

name: Woman with three hundred sixty-five children. Punished for self-righteous condemnation of unchaste girl.
bibliographies: *Taylor Notes and Queries No. 251 (Feb., 1923) 96; *K. Nyrop Grevinden med de 365 Born (København, 1909); Zs. f. Vksk. XIX 469; Child II 67f., IV 463b.; *Köhler Lais der Marie de France 2d ed. xc; *DeCock Volkssage 9ff.
additional_description: Punished for self-righteous condemnation of unchaste girl.

name: Self-righteous tailor in heaven expelled. Throws God's footstool at an old woman thief on earth.
bibliographies: *Type 800; BP I 342; Wesselski Bebel I 126 No. 19.
additional_description: Throws God's footstool at an old woman thief on earth.

name: The beggar on the cross in place of Christ. Is made to leave the cross for his impatience concerning the sinners.
additional_description: Is made to leave the cross for his impatience concerning the sinners.

name: Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Wild animal finds his liberty better than tame animal's ease.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Tame bird and wild bird. The tame bird tells the wild one to look about him. He is shot.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
additional_description: The tame bird tells the wild one to look about him. He is shot.

name: Wild ass envies tame ass until he sees his burdens.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 59 (ST 198), 124 (ST 328); Halm Aesop No. 321.

name: Wolf prefers liberty and hunger to dog's servitude and plenty.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
bibliographies: *Type 201; Crane Vitry 221 No. 217; Wienert FFC LVI 61 (ET 238), 124 (ST 326); Halm Aesop No. 278; Jacobs Aesop 206 No. 28; Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 433; Scala Celi 76b No. 435; Italian Novella: Rotunda; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Parrot prefers cold wet nest in freedom to luxury in royal palace.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Ass is jealous of the horse until he learns better.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Ass jealous of horse, but sees horse later working in a mill.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Ass jealous of war-horse until he sees him wounded.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 58 (ET 191), 124 (ST 327); Halm Aesop No. 328; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Mule carrying corn escapes while one carrying gold is robbed.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.

name: Lean dogs envy arena-dog his fatness. Later see their error.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
additional_description: Later see their error.

name: Calf pities draft ox: is taken to slaughter, ox spared.
section: L450. Proud animal less fortunate than humble.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 61 (ET 230), 57 (ET 185), *92 (ST 44), 124 (ST 331); Halm Aesop No. 113.

name: Pride brought low – miscellaneous.
Stag scorns his legs but is proud of his horns. Caught by his horns in trees.
bibliographies: *Type 77; Wienert FFC LVI 65 (ET 285), 140 (ST 465); Halm Aesop No. 128; *Crane Vitry 254 No. 274; Herbert III 23; Jacobs Aesop 206 No. 25.
additional_description: Caught by his horns in trees.

name: Fox destroys nest of bird who boasts of nest's warmth.
The mule's double ancestry. When well fed says, "My mother was a thoroughbred horse." Later, hard worked, says, "My father was a miserable ass."
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 64 (ET 275), 94 (ST 80); Halm Aesop No. 157.
additional_description: When well fed says, "My mother was a thoroughbred horse. " Later, hard worked, says, "My father was a miserable ass.

name: The man scorns the storm: killed by it.
Zeus smites Capaneus while he is climbing a ladder.
Pride or wealth of man brought low by actions of gods.
Oil lamp blown out: had thought that it outshone stars.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 75 (ET 409), 93 (ST 64); Halm Aesop No. 285.

name: Jackal singing about his deeds falls down from tree and is eaten by alligator.
Gnats having overcome lion are in turn killed by spider.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 48 (ET 75), 92 (ST 57); Halm Aesop No. 234.

name: Men too prosperous (happy): things are made more difficult.
Men are too rich: greedy gods created to impoverish them.
Men are too rich: gold sent below ground.
Men are too rich: weeds created to spoil their harvests.
Men are too proud: snakes created.
Men are fearless: tiger made to frighten them.
Men enjoy themselves too much: disease created.
Abused youngest son (by his elder brothers)
bibliographies: Polynesia: Kirtley: Tuamotus: Stimson, n.d., T-G. 3/403, T-G. 3/615.
additional_description: Kirtley .+ entry under L10.2

name: Abused half-brother as hero
additional_description: Kirtley .+ entry under L113.1

Cave abode of hero
